core_mechanism: parametric amplification of a field's zero-point (vacuum) fluctuations by an externally time-modulated boundary condition or parameter, i.e. parametric resonance that converts the modulation energy into real field quanta
brief_reason: The abstract is only a brief review naming the 'Dynamical Casimir Effect' and its proposed experimental realizations without detailing equations, but that effect is precisely the parametric excitation of a field's vacuum fluctuations driven by time-dependent boundaries, so that is the most specific defensible transferable machinery.
